definition: The dollar cycle is the primary exogenous driver of EM sovereign stress, operating through three positively correlated channels: balance-sheet effects on ~$13T USD-denominated liabilities outside the US, tightening global financial conditions, and commodity price suppression. A 100bp US term premium shock generates ~200-250bp equivalent EM tightening due to convexity.
evidence: Every major EM crisis cluster preceded by sustained dollar strengthening (1982, 1997-98, 2013-15, 2021-22)
2013 Taper Tantrum: UST +130bp, EM spreads +100bp, EM FX -5-15%, reserve losses ~$100B
EM local-currency debt shift (45% to 70%) has disguised rather than eliminated vulnerability
open_questions: Has the shift to EM local-currency debt fundamentally changed or merely disguised vulnerability?
What is the threshold of dollar strength that triggers EM stress cascades?
How does the convexity in EM response manifest in portfolio flows vs. fundamentals?
